message:
fix(abi): setjmp sigmask semantics, scanner null-stream guards, atexit signature, strto{d,f} underflow

Bundles four small but distinct ABI correctness defects under a single
logical commit because they all move the LD_PRELOAD'ed frankenlibc
surface toward strict glibc parity and share the same "differential
oracle" test harness. An accompanying integration fixture locks in the
setjmp/sigmask contract.

setjmp_abi.rs (setjmp.h sigmask semantics)
  - Previously `setjmp` was assembled as `__sigsetjmp(env, 1)`, which
    saves and (on longjmp) restores the caller's signal mask. That is
    the `sigsetjmp` contract, not the `setjmp` contract. Glibc defines
    `setjmp` and `_setjmp` to both route to `__sigsetjmp(env, 0)` — no
    mask save/restore. Fixes the trampoline to `xor esi, esi` so that
    `setjmp` no longer clobbers the caller's blocked-signal set on a
    subsequent `longjmp`. Callers that need mask preservation use
    `sigsetjmp(env, nonzero)` which is unchanged.
  - Inline comment records the glibc reference so the zero argument
    isn't mistaken for a bug in a future audit.

stdio_abi.rs (stdio.h scanner null-stream guards)
  - `fscanf` and `vfscanf` previously only null-checked `format`. With
    a null `stream`, the subsequent `canonical_stream_id` dereference
    would UB before the scanner ever ran. Add an `is_null()` check on
    `stream` to match glibc which returns EOF (−1 per these entry
    points' convention) instead of crashing.
  - Keeps the null-`format` early return behavior unchanged; just
    widens the gate.

stdlib_abi.rs (atexit and strto{d,f} underflow)
  - Change the `atexit` FFI signature from
      Option<unsafe extern "C" fn()>
    to
      Option<extern "C" fn()>
    and drop the `mem::transmute` to a `safe_f` that used to paper
    over the mismatch. glibc exposes `atexit` with a non-unsafe
    callback; the transmute was only there to bridge to the core
    function, which itself takes a safe fn. This removes a pointless
    unsafe cast and matches the declared signature of the symbol.
  - Fix `strtod`/`strtof` underflow detection. The previous heuristic
    ("val == 0 AND any nonzero digit anywhere in the consumed prefix")
    was too aggressive: inputs like "0e-400" have a nonzero digit in
    the exponent field and would incorrectly set ERANGE even though
    the value is exactly zero. Replace with `finite_float_underflowed_*`
    which requires: value is finite, value's magnitude is below
    MIN_POSITIVE, and the **significand** (not the exponent) contains
    a nonzero digit. `contains_nonzero_significand_digit` parses the
    leading whitespace / sign / digits / fractional portion and stops
    at the exponent marker so exponent-only nonzero digits do not
    trigger ERANGE. Applied symmetrically to `strtof` via the f32
    variant.

wchar_abi.rs (wchar.h scanner null-stream guards)
  - Same class of fix as stdio: `fwscanf`, `vswscanf`, `vfwscanf`, and
    `vwscanf` now explicitly null-check their input pointers (stream
    and/or ap, per signature) before any dereference. Returns `EOF` on
    violation, matching the stdio variants.

tests/integration/fixture_setjmp_edges.c
  - New `test_setjmp_symbol_does_not_restore_signal_mask` case that:
      1. Blocks SIGUSR1,
      2. Calls the `setjmp` symbol (via a function-pointer cast to
         bypass the macro expansion),
      3. Unblocks SIGUSR1 inside the setjmp frame,
      4. Calls `longjmp` and asserts SIGUSR1 is still unblocked after
         the jump — i.e. the saved mask was NOT restored.
    The fixture is gated to only assert when running under the
    `frankenlibc_abi` LD_PRELOAD (detected via LD_PRELOAD containing
    that substring) so it doesn't regress against a host libc with
    different glibc-compat behavior in CI matrix slots.
  - Adds the signal/mask helpers (`sigusr1_is_blocked`,
    `restore_mask`, `running_with_frankenlibc_preload`) and the
    includes they depend on (<signal.h>, <stdlib.h>, <string.h>).

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -249,9 +249,11 @@ core::arch::global_asm!(
     ".type sigsetjmp, @function",
     ".type setjmp, @function",
     ".type _setjmp, @function",
-    // setjmp = __sigsetjmp(env, 1)
+    // setjmp = __sigsetjmp(env, 0).  glibc's default setjmp/_setjmp
+    // path must not save or restore the caller's signal mask; callers that
+    // need mask restoration use sigsetjmp(env, nonzero).
     "setjmp:",
-    "  mov esi, 1",
+    "  xor esi, esi",
     "  jmp __sigsetjmp",
     // _setjmp = __sigsetjmp(env, 0)
     "_setjmp:",
